title: Income and Expenditure Statements
description: Prepare a comprehensive income and expenditure statement that calculates gross profit, tax, and net profit using percentage formulas.
objectives: Create a structured income and expenditure worksheet
Calculate income from sales and services
Compute gross profit and net profit
Format the statement for board presentation
content:
Income and Expenditure Statements

An income and expenditure statement (also called a profit and loss statement) shows how much money a business earned and spent over a period. It is one of the most important financial documents in any organization. As an administrative professional, you may be asked to prepare this statement for monthly board meetings or annual audits.

Structure of the Statement

The statement has two main parts:

  1. Income: Sales, after-sale services, and other revenue.
  2. Expenditure: Rent, salaries, raw materials, water and electricity, miscellaneous.

After calculating total income and total expenditure, you subtract to find Gross Profit (or Loss). Then you calculate tax as a percentage of gross profit. Finally, you subtract tax to find Net Profit.

6-Month Income and Expenditure Table

CategoryJanFebMarAprMayJun
Sales990,000880,000910,000890,000780,000900,000
After-Sale Services (50%)495,000440,000455,000445,000390,000450,000
Total Income1,485,0001,320,0001,365,0001,335,0001,170,0001,350,000
Rent (8%)79,20070,40072,80071,20062,40072,000
Salaries (10%)99,00088,00091,00089,00078,00090,000
Raw Materials (18%)178,200158,400163,800160,200140,400162,000
Water & Elec (8%)79,20070,40072,80071,20062,40072,000
Miscellaneous (3%)29,70026,40027,30026,70023,40027,000
Total Expenditure465,300413,600427,700418,300366,600423,000
Gross Profit1,019,700906,400937,300916,700803,400927,000
Tax (4%)40,78836,25637,49236,66832,13637,080
Net Profit978,912870,144899,808880,032771,264889,920

Formulas Used

  • After-Sale Services = Sales × 0.50
  • Total Income = Sales + After-Sale Services
  • Rent = Sales × 0.08
  • Salaries = Sales × 0.10
  • Raw Materials = Sales × 0.18
  • Water & Electricity = Sales × 0.08
  • Miscellaneous = Sales × 0.03
  • Total Expenditure = SUM of all expenses
  • Gross Profit = Total Income − Total Expenditure
  • Tax = Gross Profit × 0.04
  • Net Profit = Gross Profit − Tax

Workplace Scenario

You are the finance assistant at a manufacturing company. At the end of each quarter, you must prepare the income and expenditure statement for the board. You enter the monthly sales figures. Excel automatically calculates after-sale services, all expenses, gross profit, tax (4%), and net profit using formulas. You create a column chart comparing monthly net profit. You format the statement professionally and export it as a PDF for the board meeting. The board uses your statement to decide on expansion plans for the next quarter.

downloads.title: Income Expenditure Template
downloads.description: Excel income and expenditure statement with formulas

exercise: Create a 6-month income and expenditure statement in Excel. Enter sales for each month: Jan 500,000, Feb 450,000, Mar 480,000, Apr 520,000, May 490,000, Jun 550,000. Calculate after-sale services at 50% of sales. Calculate expenses: Rent (8%), Salaries (10%), Raw Materials (15%), Utilities (7%), Miscellaneous (3%). Calculate Total Income, Total Expenditure, Gross Profit, Tax (5%), and Net Profit using formulas. Create a column chart showing monthly net profit. Save as Income_Statement.xlsx.

quiz.question: What is Gross Profit?
quiz.explanation: Gross Profit is calculated by subtracting total expenditure from total income.

quiz.question: If sales are 1,000,000 and after-sale services are 50% of sales, what is total income?
Why should you use formulas instead of typing each calculated value?
options: If you change the sales figure, all calculations update automatically
quiz.explanation: Formulas ensure that when you change a source value like sales, every dependent calculation updates automatically without manual re-entry.

quiz.question: Which chart type is best for comparing monthly net profit over 6 months?
quiz.explanation: A column chart makes it easy to compare values across different months side by side.
